phases.name: UnixInstant newtype + completeness
phases.description: Introduce overdrive_core::wall_clock::UnixInstant — a Duration-since-UNIX_EPOCH newtype that satisfies the project's strict newtype completeness rules (FromStr/Display/Serde + validating constructors) and the mandatory proptest call sites (newtype roundtrip + rkyv roundtrip). This phase is the foundation: every later step depends on the type existing with its full surface so call sites can adopt it without churn. No reconciler logic is touched in this phase.

steps.name: UnixInstant newtype with rkyv derives, arithmetic impls, and constructors
steps.description: Create crates/overdrive-core/src/wall_clock.rs containing the UnixInstant(Duration) newtype per the research doc § 'Recommended call-site shape'. Derive Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, rkyv::Archive, rkyv::Serialize, rkyv::Deserialize. Provide constructors: from_clock<C: Clock + ?Sized>(&C) -> Self (production entry), const fn from_unix_duration(Duration) -> Self (test/hydrate entry), const fn as_unix_duration(self) -> Duration (accessor). Implement Add<Duration> for UnixInstant -> Self and Sub<Self> for UnixInstant -> Duration (saturating to Duration::ZERO on negative diff per research doc). Wire `pub mod wall_clock;` and `pub use wall_clock::UnixInstant;` into crates/overdrive-core/src/lib.rs so call sites can `use overdrive_core::UnixInstant;` per import-style rule in development.md. No reconciler call site changes in this step — only the type and its public surface land. Test file location follows the established overdrive-core newtype-test convention: behaviour tests for newtypes (arithmetic, constructors, roundtrip) live under `crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/` alongside peers like `core_newtype_roundtrip.rs`, `core_newtype_validation.rs`, `content_hash_cert_serial.rs`, and `extended_newtype_completeness.rs`. There is no `tests/unit/` lane in this crate; colocated `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` blocks are not the convention either. The arithmetic test for UnixInstant therefore lives at `crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/unix_instant_arithmetic.rs` — port-to-port at domain scope, since the newtype's public signature IS its driving port.
steps.criteria: GIVEN crates/overdrive-core/src/wall_clock.rs does not exist WHEN the step is implemented THEN the file exists with `pub struct UnixInstant(Duration)` carrying the full derive list (Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, rkyv::Archive, rkyv::Serialize, rkyv::Deserialize), the three constructors (from_clock, from_unix_duration, as_unix_duration), and the two arithmetic impls (Add<Duration> for UnixInstant, Sub<Self> for UnixInstant -> Duration); AND the type is re-exported from overdrive_core; AND a unit test verifies that `UnixInstant::from_unix_duration(Duration::from_secs(10)) + Duration::from_secs(5) == UnixInstant::from_unix_duration(Duration::from_secs(15))`; AND a unit test verifies `UnixInstant::from_unix_duration(Duration::from_secs(15)) - UnixInstant::from_unix_duration(Duration::from_secs(10)) == Duration::from_secs(5)` and that the saturating subtraction returns Duration::ZERO when the right-hand side is greater; AND a unit test verifies `UnixInstant::from_clock(&clock).as_unix_duration() == clock.unix_now()` against a SimClock fixture; AND `cargo check -p overdrive-core` succeeds on Linux (or `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo check -p overdrive-core` on macOS).

steps.name: UnixInstant FromStr/Display/Serde completeness + proptest roundtrips (newtype + rkyv)
steps.description: Satisfy the project's strict newtype-completeness rules in development.md § 'Newtype completeness': implement `impl Display for UnixInstant` whose canonical form is `<seconds>.<nanos>` with exactly 9 nanos digits, zero-padded (e.g. `1700000000.000000123`, `1700000000.000000000`); `impl FromStr for UnixInstant` returning `Result<Self, ParseError>` with a structured ParseError enum (variants: Empty, MalformedDecimal, NanosOverflow). FromStr accepts any decimal form — `1700000000` (no fractional component), `1700000000.0` (short fractional), `1700000000.000000123` (full 9-digit fractional) — and normalises them all to the canonical 9-digit form on parse; the resulting UnixInstant re-emits the canonical form via Display. Roundtrip property: `UnixInstant::from_str(&u.to_string()) == Ok(u)` for every valid `u`. Serde Serialize/Deserialize delegate to Display/FromStr exactly so the JSON form matches the human-typed form. Add the two mandatory proptest roundtrips listed in testing.md § 'Property-based testing (proptest) — Mandatory call sites': (1) Display/FromStr roundtrip — every valid Duration must round-trip bit-equivalent through `unix_instant.to_string().parse::<UnixInstant>()`, and every invalid string must produce a structured ParseError variant; (2) rkyv archive→access→deserialise→equal roundtrip — `rkyv::to_bytes(&unix_instant)` followed by archive access and deserialise must equal the original. Both proptests live in the dedicated acceptance test file and share an Arbitrary impl that lives next to the type per development.md (`crates/overdrive-core/src/wall_clock.rs` exposes the Arbitrary impl behind `#[cfg(any(test, feature = ...))]` if needed, or directly in the test file if the workspace convention is local-only). The mutation-testing § 'Mandatory targets' rule applies to FromStr accept/reject branches — every variant of ParseError must have at least one test that flips on the mutation, parametrized into the same roundtrip proptest where possible.
steps.criteria: GIVEN UnixInstant exists from step 01-01 WHEN this step is implemented THEN UnixInstant impls Display emitting the canonical form `<seconds>.<nanos>` with exactly 9 nanos digits zero-padded (e.g. `1700000000.000000123`, `1700000000.000000000`), FromStr returning Result<Self, ParseError> that accepts any decimal form (`1700000000`, `1700000000.0`, `1700000000.000000123`) and normalises to the 9-digit canonical form, and serde Serialize/Deserialize matching Display/FromStr exactly; AND a unit test verifies `UnixInstant::from_unix_duration(Duration::new(1_700_000_000, 123)).to_string() == "1700000000.000000123"` and `UnixInstant::from_unix_duration(Duration::from_secs(1_700_000_000)).to_string() == "1700000000.000000000"`; AND a unit test verifies `"1700000000".parse::<UnixInstant>().unwrap() == "1700000000.0".parse::<UnixInstant>().unwrap() == "1700000000.000000000".parse::<UnixInstant>().unwrap()`; AND a proptest with PROPTEST_CASES default (1024) verifies the roundtrip property `UnixInstant::from_str(&u.to_string()) == Ok(u)` over arbitrary Duration values up to u64::MAX seconds; AND a proptest verifies FromStr rejects empty strings, non-numeric input, and overflowing nanos with the corresponding structured ParseError variant; AND a proptest verifies rkyv archive→access→deserialise→equal roundtrip over arbitrary Duration values; AND a proptest verifies serde_json roundtrip equivalence (serialize → deserialize → equals original); AND `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-core --test acceptance` (via `cargo xtask lima run --` on macOS) passes; AND `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-core --file crates/overdrive-core/src/wall_clock.rs` reports kill rate ≥ 80%.

phases.name: TickContext.now_unix + reconciler shape change + runtime construction
phases.description: Wire UnixInstant into the reconciler runtime contract. TickContext gains a `now_unix: UnixInstant` field (additive — `now: Instant` stays for in-process budget/deadline arithmetic per the comment at reconciler.rs:177). Introduce the `backoff_for_attempt` const fn (degenerate Phase 1 policy returning RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION regardless of attempt). Rename and retype JobLifecycleView::next_attempt_at to last_failure_seen_at: BTreeMap<AllocationId, UnixInstant>. Update JobLifecycle::reconcile read site to recompute the deadline as `tick.now_unix < seen_at + backoff_for_attempt(restart_count)` and the write site to persist `tick.now_unix` (the failure observation timestamp), not the precomputed deadline. Update the runtime construction site at reconciler_runtime.rs:248 to populate now_unix via UnixInstant::from_clock(&*state.clock) once per tick. ESR purity contract is preserved — no Instant::now() / SystemTime::now() reachable from inside reconcile. This phase changes the production call shape; the test fixture sweep is Phase 3.

steps.name: TickContext.now_unix field + backoff_for_attempt const fn + runtime construction site
steps.description: Add `now_unix: UnixInstant` to TickContext at crates/overdrive-core/src/reconciler.rs:178-186 (additive; place between `now` and `tick` with a docstring distinguishing wall-clock/persistable from monotonic/in-process per the research doc's TickContext shape). Introduce a `pub const fn backoff_for_attempt(_attempt: u32) -> Duration { RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION }` adjacent to RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION (crates/overdrive-core/src/reconciler.rs:961). The leading underscore on `_attempt` is deliberate — it makes the unused-parameter intent explicit (degenerate Phase 1 policy; the parameter exists so call sites stay stable when operator-configurable policy lands later, per issue #141 'Out' section). Document inline that operator-configurable policy is Phase 2+ scope. Update the runtime construction site at crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconciler_runtime.rs:248 to populate `now_unix: UnixInstant::from_clock(&*state.clock)` exactly once per tick (single snapshot — same evaluation must see one consistent now_unix). No reconciler logic changes yet; this step lands the field, the policy lookup function, and the runtime wiring so step 02-02 can swap the JobLifecycleView shape without breaking the build.
steps.criteria: GIVEN TickContext currently has no now_unix field WHEN this step is implemented THEN TickContext at crates/overdrive-core/src/reconciler.rs carries `pub now_unix: UnixInstant` in addition to the existing now/tick/deadline fields, with a docstring distinguishing wall-clock from monotonic; AND `pub const fn backoff_for_attempt(_attempt: u32) -> Duration { RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION }` exists adjacent to RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION with the leading-underscore parameter (signature is exact — the function body is the single literal `RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION` expression); AND crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconciler_runtime.rs:248 constructs the TickContext with `now_unix: UnixInstant::from_clock(&*state.clock)` exactly once per tick; AND a unit test against the runtime verifies the constructed TickContext's now_unix equals the SimClock's unix_now() at construction time; AND a unit test verifies `backoff_for_attempt(0) == backoff_for_attempt(4) == RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION` over the full RESTART_BACKOFF_CEILING range (parametrised); AND `cargo check --workspace` succeeds with the additive field (every existing TickContext construction site is updated to populate now_unix); AND no production code in crates/overdrive-core or crates/overdrive-control-plane references `Instant::now()` or `SystemTime::now()` inside any reconcile method body (dst-lint gate passes).

steps.name: JobLifecycleView retype + reconcile read/write sites recompute from inputs
steps.description: Rename and retype `JobLifecycleView::next_attempt_at: BTreeMap<AllocationId, Instant>` at crates/overdrive-core/src/reconciler.rs:1310-1331 to `last_failure_seen_at: BTreeMap<AllocationId, UnixInstant>`. Update the docstring to reflect that the field carries the *observation timestamp of the last failure* — an input — not a derived deadline. Update the read site at crates/overdrive-core/src/reconciler.rs:1134-1139 to recompute the deadline: look up `last_failure_seen_at`, look up the corresponding `restart_count` from `restart_counts` (defaulting to 0 if absent), call `backoff_for_attempt(restart_count)`, and compare `tick.now_unix < seen_at + backoff`. Update the write site at crates/overdrive-core/src/reconciler.rs:1170-1172 to insert `tick.now_unix` (the observation timestamp) into last_failure_seen_at, not a precomputed deadline. Per issue #141 acceptance: a no-op policy change in code (re-running the same RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION via backoff_for_attempt) must produce identical reconcile output across restart. ESR purity contract preserved: `reconcile` remains sync, takes only (desired, actual, view, tick), reads tick.now_unix not Instant::now(). Per issue #141 'Out' scope: this is purely a code refactor — no schema migration, no on-disk format change (Phase 1 has no persisted reconciler memory). The acceptance test exercises the JobLifecycle reconciler through its driving port (port-to-port at domain scope per nw-tdd-methodology) and asserts on observable reconcile outputs (returned Actions + NextView), not internal state.
steps.criteria: GIVEN JobLifecycleView::next_attempt_at: BTreeMap<AllocationId, Instant> exists today WHEN this step is implemented THEN JobLifecycleView at crates/overdrive-core/src/reconciler.rs carries `pub last_failure_seen_at: BTreeMap<AllocationId, UnixInstant>` (renamed and retyped), with no remaining `Instant`-typed field; AND JobLifecycle::reconcile read site recomputes the deadline as `tick.now_unix < seen_at + backoff_for_attempt(restart_count)` and the write site inserts `tick.now_unix` into last_failure_seen_at; AND a behavioural acceptance test exercises JobLifecycle::reconcile through its public signature with a fixture seeded with restart_counts=2 and last_failure_seen_at=UnixInstant(t0), and asserts that calling reconcile at tick.now_unix = t0 + (RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION - 1ns) returns no Actions (backoff window not elapsed), while calling at tick.now_unix = t0 + RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION returns the expected restart Action; AND a behavioural acceptance test verifies the restart-survival property: persisting (restart_counts, last_failure_seen_at) into a fresh JobLifecycleView and calling reconcile against a TickContext with the same tick.now_unix and tick.tick values (all TickContext inputs held constant, not just wall-clock) produces identical Actions to the pre-persistence reconcile; AND no `Instant` reference remains anywhere in JobLifecycle::reconcile body or its helpers; AND the dst-lint gate confirms no Instant::now() / SystemTime::now() is reachable from inside reconcile; AND `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-core --test acceptance` (via `cargo xtask lima run --` on macOS) passes with the existing JobLifecycle acceptance suite green; AND `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-core --file crates/overdrive-core/src/reconciler.rs` reports kill rate ≥ 80% on the changed sites.

phases.name: Test fixture sweep + DST acceptance suite green
phases.description: After Phase 2 lands the production call shape, every existing test fixture that constructs a TickContext or seeds a JobLifecycleView needs the now_unix field and the renamed last_failure_seen_at field. Bundle this into discrete steps so each remains step-self-contained per the TDD discipline: one step updates the core/sim/control-plane `fresh_tick` test helpers in lockstep (they share the same shape and must stay coherent), and a follow-up step verifies the full DST acceptance suite passes against the new shape — including the two pinned suites (reconciler_is_pure_with_job_lifecycle.rs, runtime_convergence_loop.rs) — and runs the per-PR mutation gate end-to-end.

steps.name: fresh_tick helpers + JobLifecycleView fixtures across core/sim/control-plane acceptance suites
steps.description: Update every `fresh_tick(now: Instant)` helper to the canonical signature `fresh_tick(now: Instant, now_unix: UnixInstant) -> TickContext`. This is the single, uniform shape used across all five enumerated test files (no shared-default fallback, no per-file variation). The known set of files (per Grep against fresh_tick on the working tree): crates/overdrive-sim/tests/acceptance/reconciler_is_pure_with_job_lifecycle.rs, crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/job_lifecycle_reconcile_branches.rs, crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/first_fit_place_branches.rs, crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/exec_reconciler_purity.rs, crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/runtime_registers_noop_heartbeat.rs. Every helper definition AND every call site in those files takes the second `now_unix: UnixInstant` parameter explicitly — tests that do not exercise wall-clock pass `UnixInstant::from_unix_duration(Duration::from_secs(0))` at the call site so the helper signature stays uniform. Additionally, every test that seeds a JobLifecycleView with the old `next_attempt_at` field must be updated to use `last_failure_seen_at` with the equivalent semantic (the timestamp of the last failure, not the precomputed deadline; existing `tick.now + RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION` write expressions become `tick.now_unix` and the corresponding read expectations change from 'compare against deadline' to 'compare against seen_at + backoff_for_attempt(count)'). This is a mechanical sweep — no production logic change. The step is self-contained: it neither lands new behaviour nor removes existing test coverage; it adapts the existing fixtures to the Phase 2 shape so the suite compiles and passes.
steps.criteria: GIVEN Phase 2 has landed the TickContext.now_unix field and the JobLifecycleView retype WHEN this step is implemented THEN every fresh_tick helper in the five enumerated test files has the canonical signature `fresh_tick(now: Instant, now_unix: UnixInstant) -> TickContext` (uniform across all files; no per-file variation); AND every call site to fresh_tick in those files passes the now_unix argument explicitly (tests that do not exercise wall-clock pass `UnixInstant::from_unix_duration(Duration::from_secs(0))`); AND every test that previously seeded `next_attempt_at` now seeds `last_failure_seen_at` with the equivalent observation-timestamp semantic; AND `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests` (via `cargo xtask lima run --` on macOS) compiles cleanly and the existing acceptance scenarios that were passing before this PR continue to pass; AND no test references `JobLifecycleView::next_attempt_at` (the field no longer exists); AND `git grep 'next_attempt_at' -- crates/` returns zero hits in test files; AND no test in the five enumerated files uses `Instant::now()` or `SystemTime::now()` for backoff-relevant assertions (tests source time from fresh_tick, which sources it from a SimClock fixture).

steps.name: DST acceptance suite green + per-PR mutation gate end-to-end
steps.description: Final gate: the two DST acceptance suites pinned in issue #141's acceptance section must pass against the new shape — reconciler_is_pure_with_job_lifecycle.rs (proves ESR purity contract: reconcile is pure over (desired, actual, view, tick) with no Instant::now()/SystemTime::now() reachable) and runtime_convergence_loop.rs (proves the runtime construction site populates now_unix correctly and the convergence loop converges with the new shape). The runtime_convergence_loop suite must include or be extended with an assertion exercising the issue #141 'no-op policy change produces identical reconcile output across restart' acceptance bullet: simulate a control-plane restart (drop the JobLifecycleView, re-hydrate from (restart_counts, last_failure_seen_at)) and verify the next reconcile tick produces the same Actions as the pre-restart tick when backoff_for_attempt is unchanged. The per-PR mutation gate runs end-to-end against the diff scope: `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests` must report kill rate ≥ 80% across the touched files (UnixInstant FromStr branches, JobLifecycle::reconcile read/write sites, backoff_for_attempt). All quality gates G1–G9 from the quality-framework skill pass before commit (G1: exactly one acceptance test active during RED_ACCEPTANCE; G2: acceptance test fails for valid reason; G3: unit test fails on assertion not panic; G4: no mocks inside hexagon; G5: business language in tests; G6: all green at GREEN; G7: 100% passing before COMMIT; G8: test budget honored; G9: no test modifications during GREEN/REFACTOR).
steps.criteria: GIVEN Phase 2 has landed the production shape and step 03-01 has updated every fixture WHEN this step is verified THEN crates/overdrive-sim/tests/acceptance/reconciler_is_pure_with_job_lifecycle.rs passes against the new shape, including its existing assertions that no Instant::now()/SystemTime::now() is reachable from inside reconcile; AND crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/runtime_convergence_loop.rs passes (or is extended with) an explicit assertion exercising issue #141's 'no-op policy change produces identical reconcile output across restart' bullet — i.e. dropping and rehydrating JobLifecycleView from (restart_counts, last_failure_seen_at) yields a reconcile output bit-equivalent to the pre-restart tick when backoff_for_attempt is unchanged; AND `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests` (Linux/Lima) reports zero failures across the workspace; AND `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests` reports kill rate ≥ 80% across the diff; AND quality gates G1–G9 from quality-framework pass — G1: exactly one acceptance test active during RED_ACCEPTANCE; G2: acceptance test fails for valid reason (business logic, not import/syntax/setup error); G3: unit test fails on assertion, not panic; G4: no mocks inside hexagon (only sim adapters at port boundaries — SimClock for time); G5: business language in tests verified (test names describe domain behaviour, not implementation mechanics); G6: all green at GREEN phase; G7: 100% passing before COMMIT (acceptance + unit + integration; formatting via `cargo fmt --check`; static analysis via `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --features integration-tests -- -D warnings`); G8: test budget honored (≤ 2x distinct behaviours); G9: no test modifications during GREEN/REFACTOR; AND the post-GREEN wiring check (git diff --name-only) shows every file in this step's files_to_modify is present in the diff (no Fixture Theater — production sites in Phase 1/2 must be the ones implementing the behaviour, not Phase 3 fixtures).

validation.note: CONDITIONALLY_APPROVED with 6 clarifications; all patched and re-validated (validator exit 0)
